title: Cybersecurity for Small Business

description: A 30-minute walkthrough for NYC small businesses claiming or rebuilding their Google Business Profile so neighbors can actually find the shop.

description: Six early warning signs that the freelance developer or agency you hired is going to disappear mid-project — and what to do before it happens.

description: One dead laptop should not erase your customer list. The 3-2-1 backup rule in plain English, plus seven steps a busy NYC shop owner can do this week.

title: What to Do When Your Business Wi-Fi Keeps Dropping
description: Shop Wi-Fi that keeps dropping is one of three things: the line, the router, or a crowded network. Here is how to find which one, and how to fix it.

title: How to Own Your Domain Name, Not Your Web Guy
description: If your web developer registered your domain, you may not control your own name online. Here is how to check who owns it and take it back, step by step.

description: Reply to every Google review, good and bad. Plain-English templates for happy, angry, unfair, and fake reviews, and why replies help you show up locally.

description: AI assistants now answer "best tailor near me." How a small NYC business becomes the answer: clear pages, consistent details, real reviews, no tricks.

description: Chains rank first because corporate teams feed Google perfect data all day. Here is how that machine works, and how one shop wins its own neighborhood.

title: Your Website Is Your Hardest-Working Employee
description: Your website works nights, weekends, and holidays answering customers while you sleep. Here is how to train it like staff, and check its work monthly.

title: The Pen and Paper Tax: What It Costs You in Time
description: Paper, memory, and one big spreadsheet quietly tax your shop in hours and mistakes: double bookings, lost messages, retyping. When paper is fine, too.

title: AI Is Already Answering Questions About Your Business
description: AI assistants describe your shop whether you join in or not, reading your site, Google profile, and reviews. Here is how to own the sources they quote.

title: Why Small Business Tech Should Be Boring
description: Shiny tools break on Saturdays. The chains win with dull systems that open, ring, charge, and back up daily. Reliability is the real premium for shops.

description: What happens when a NYC owner books our free Tech Audit: what we look at, what you get in writing, and why there is no sales pitch at the end of it.

title: Signs Your POS Is About to Fail

description: Real questions NYC small-business owners ask us about AI, with straight answers. What it can do for your shop, what it cannot, and what to ignore.

description: Accessibility demand letters keep landing on NYC small businesses. What web accessibility really means, how to check your own site, and how we build it.

description: WordPress, Webflow, or a custom-built site: all three can work for a serious NYC business. An honest look at upkeep, lock-in, and who fixes it when it breaks.

title: Toast vs Clover for NYC Bars and Small Restaurants
description: Toast was built for bars: fast tabs, pre-auth, offline mode. Clover is flexible but support depends on the reseller. How to choose a POS that survives Friday.
